input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML â€” Use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension

Replace every hardcoded color with a binding that specifies both `Light` and `Dark` values:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#512BD4, Dark=#BB86FC}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

MAUI reads the device's current `AppTheme` and picks `Light` or `Dark` automatically. When the user toggles their system theme, every `AppThemeBinding` updates live â€” no event handling needed.

### C# alternative â€” `SetAppTheme` extension method

The equivalent in code-behind or any C# context:

```csharp
// On any BindableObject, call SetAppTheme<T>(property, lightValue, darkValue)
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme<Color>(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#000000"),   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0")); // dark

var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme<Color>(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

### Scaling up â€” use resource dictionaries

For real apps, define colors once in `App.xaml` and reference them by key:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}</Color>
</Application.Resources>

<!-- Any page -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}">
    <Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryText}" />
</ContentPage>
```

### Key points

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (OS setting) |
| **Live update** | Yes â€” reacts instantly to system theme changes |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme` to force `Light`/`Dark` |
| **Unspecified** | If theme is `Unspecified`, the `Light` value is used as fallback |
